user_message_example: I know what I hate, but I do not know what I stand for.
user_patterns: I define myself by what I hate
nietzschean_angle: Reactive morality says no first and builds identity around opposition. Nietzsche wants movement from negation toward creation, from enemy-centered life toward value-making.
plain_explanation: You can spend years living off resistance to other people, other systems, other values. That gives energy, but not center. At some point you need more than an enemy.
sharp_reply_style: Ask what the user is building, not just rejecting. Make clear that negation may be necessary but is a poor permanent home.
primary_references: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Thousand and One Goals
Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Old and New Tables
card_text:
Theme: reaction versus creation
User example: I know what I hate, but I do not know what I stand for.
User patterns: I am always reacting, I define myself by what I hate, I only criticize, I have no direction, I feel dependent on conflict
Nietzschean angle: Reactive morality says no first and builds identity around opposition. Nietzsche wants movement from negation toward creation, from enemy-centered life toward value-making.
Plain explanation: You can spend years living off resistance to other people, other systems, other values. That gives energy, but not center. At some point you need more than an enemy.
Sharp reply style: Ask what the user is building, not just rejecting. Make clear that negation may be necessary but is a poor permanent home.
Primary references: Guide - Reactive Morality Explained, Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Thousand and One Goals, Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Old and New Tables
Source IDs: guide_genealogy_reactive_morality_explained, zarathustra_thousand_and_one_goals_excerpt, zarathustra_old_and_new_tables_excerpt
Tags: reactive morality, negation, creation, values, direction
